extra.reason: Monorepo tree, go.mod/go.work, README/LICENSE/SECURITY/CONTRIBUTING, .gitignore. Verified: go build, go vet, gofmt clean.
package.json + pnpm-workspace.yaml + per-package stubs (11 packages), Makefile targets. Verified: pnpm install, make build/test/lint/bootstrap all pass.

8 canonical protocol schemas (adapter, event, workflow, knowledge, task, evidence, approval, flow) written to protocol/*.schema.json, draft 2020-12, grounded in plan §5.4, §7, §9-10, §12.9, §16-19. Validated: valid JSON, ajv-cli schema compile clean (date-time format warnings are ajv-cli plugin config only, not schema defects).

Created docs/adapters/manifest-format.md documenting every adapter manifest field (id, name, version, protocol, runtime, provides, permissions.network/filesystem/secrets, operations.<name>.side_effect/approval) and how the Go core supervisor uses the manifest across the §5.2 lifecycle (start -> initialize -> validate manifest -> grant capabilities -> execute -> receive result -> record evidence -> stop/reuse), referencing protocol/adapter.schema.json as canonical. Created examples/atlassian-playwright/adapter-manifest.example.yaml, the verbatim §5.4 Atlassian Cloud manifest. Validated with a throwaway node script (js-yaml + ajv/dist/2020 + ajv-formats installed via npm install --no-save in scratch dir): YAML parses cleanly and passes ajv.compile(schema)/validate(doc) against protocol/adapter.schema.json with zero errors. No protocol/*.schema.json files modified.

Wrote docs/security/threat-model.md organizing §15/§16/§24 into assets, trust boundaries (Go core trusted / TS adapters semi-trusted+sandboxed / external content untrusted), 7 threat/mitigation subsections, and residual-risk section flagging M9 items (adapter sandboxing, secret broker, tool checksums, recovery) as not yet implemented.

Created ADR-0001 through ADR-0015 under docs/architecture/ transcribing all 15 decisions from plan §25, plus a README index; each ADR follows the Status/Context/Decision/Consequences structure grounded in the plan's surrounding sections.

Go codegen: go-jsonschema as go.mod tool dep + //go:generate directive in pkg/protocol/doc.go -> types_generated.go (44 types). TS codegen: json-schema-to-typescript + json-schema-to-zod in packages/schema-types/scripts/generate.mjs -> src/generated/*.ts + *.zod.ts (interfaces + Zod validators per §5.5), tsconfig+build/typecheck wired. Makefile generate/protocol-check targets wired (protocol-check re-generates and git-diffs to catch staleness). Verified: go build/vet, tsc --noEmit, make generate/build/lint/test all pass. Note: json-schema-to-zod (StefanTerdell) is dev-tooling-only (no runtime exposure) but flagged unmaintained as of its own README; fine for now, revisit if it breaks.

Newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio. TS side: packages/adapter-sdk/src/stdio.ts (serveStdio helper) + prototypeAdapter.ts (initialize validates against generated AdapterManifestSchema, execute/sleep supports cancellation, shutdown exits). Go side: internal/adapters/stdio.go (Client: Start/Call/Shutdown/Kill, ctx-based cancellation sends a cancel notification). Integration test test/integration/adapter_stdio_test.go (build-tag integration): TestInitializeHandshake proves Go can start+handshake a TS adapter with schema validation; TestExecuteCancellation proves a 150ms ctx timeout aborts a 5s sleep op promptly. Verified: make lint/test/integration-test all pass. Satisfies M0 acceptance criteria on adapter start + cancellation/timeout.

.github/workflows/ci.yml: checkout, setup-go+setup-node/pnpm, then make bootstrap/lint/protocol-check/test/integration-test on push+PR. Scoped to what M0 actually built; broader Section 21.1 items (license scan, secret scan, multi-platform smoke build, contract tests) are later-milestone concerns (M9 hardening, adapter contract tests). Verified: workflow YAML parses correctly, and every make target it calls (bootstrap/lint/protocol-check/test/integration-test) passes locally.

All 9 deliverables closed. Acceptance criteria verified: Go starts a TS adapter (stdio prototype), both sides validate the same schemas (generated Go structs + Zod validators, manifest validated in TestInitializeHandshake), cancellation/timeout work (TestExecuteCancellation), CI detects stale generated code (protocol-check in ci.yml).

internal/workspace: Discover() walks up for .punakawan/workspace.yaml (git-style), Load() parses+validates per §6 schema (version check, duplicate/empty id checks). RepositoryPath()/PolicyPath() accessors. 5 tests against test/fixtures/workspace/. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

internal/policy: Capabilities struct matching §15.1 (filesystem/git/external/browser/execution), Level enum normalizes both §15.1 informal vocab (allowed/approval/denied) and §16.3 canonical vocab (allow/require-approval/deny/allow-with-constraints) via UnmarshalYAML. Default() is conservative (push+default-branch-write denied, external writes require approval). AllowsFilesystemWrite() enforces deny-globs (doublestar, supports **) over write-globs. 3 tests. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

internal/approvals: JSONL-backed Store (append-only per §7.5) using generated protocol.ApprovalRecord (§16.2). Append/List (full history)/Current (folds to latest per id)/Pending. 2 tests covering request->resolve lifecycle. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

internal/tools/supervisor.go: Supervisor.Run() enforces §11.4 - explicit argv only (no shell), working-dir allowlist, env allowlist (default PATH/HOME/LANG/TMPDIR), per-call timeout via context, output cap via limitedBuffer (truncates, doesn't error), process-group termination on cancel (Setpgid + SIGTERM to -pid, 5s WaitDelay before SIGKILL). POSIX-only, documented; multi-platform deferred to M9. 6 tests including a real timeout test (bug found+fixed: context-cancellation check must run before exec.ExitError check, since a SIGTERM-killed process also surfaces as ExitError). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

internal/evidence/journal.go: append-only JSONL Journal (§7.5, §19.1) using generated protocol.Event, under .punakawan/runs/<runID>/events.jsonl. Append/List. 2 tests. Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

internal/evidence/bundle.go: NewBundle() creates §17.2 skeleton (.punakawan/evidence/<runID>/<taskID>/ with logs/screenshots/traces subdirs) + Path() helper. Content-writing (task.yaml, diff.patch etc) deferred to M6 execution runtime. 2 tests. Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

internal/tools/ripgrep.go: Supervisor.Search() runs rg through the supervised execution path (§14.1). 2 tests, run for real against installed rg (skips gracefully if rg absent, e.g. in an environment without it). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

internal/tools/rtk.go: Supervisor.RunViaRTK()/RTKAvailable() run commands through the user's rtk CLI for token-compressed output (§11.4, §2.1). 2 tests, run for real against installed rtk (skips gracefully if rtk absent). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

Implemented internal/gitops/inspect.go: Inspector wraps git status/log/diff/rev-parse through tools.Supervisor (read-only, no writes). All operations go through Supervisor.Run for dir-allowlist/timeout/output-cap enforcement. Added inspect_test.go with real git repo fixtures (2 commits + uncommitted change). go build/vet/gofmt/test all clean.

internal/gitops/worktree.go: WorktreeManager implements §11.1 steps 1-3+10 (lock, clean-check, worktree+branch creation, removal) gated by approval (§16) via RequestApproval/Approve/Deny using the existing approvals.Store. No dedicated worktree-creation category exists in §16.1's approval list, so it uses ApprovalRecordOperationDestructiveFilesystemAction as the closest fit (documented in code). Non-blocking file-based per-repo lock (§3.1 workspace locking). Reuses gitops.Inspector.Status for the clean-repo check rather than duplicating git-status parsing. 5 tests: unapproved-create-fails, full request->approve->create->remove happy path, dirty-repo refusal, denied-request refusal, concurrent-lock refusal. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

internal/app/app.go (bootstrap: discover workspace, load policy, wire supervisor/approvals/inspector/worktree-manager) + cmd/punakawan/*.go (cobra CLI): 'workspace show', 'git status|log|diff|branch <repo-id>', 'worktree request|approve|deny|create|remove <repo-id> <task-id>', 'doctor'. Manually smoke-tested full worktree lifecycle end-to-end against a real ephemeral workspace+git repo, then codified as 5 in-process CLI tests + 2 app-bootstrap tests. Verified: go test/vet/gofmt, make lint/test/integration-test all pass repo-wide.

All 11 deliverables closed. Acceptance criteria verified via CLI: workspace opens with multiple repositories (workspace show), repositories inspected without writing (git status/log/diff/branch, read-only through Supervisor), isolated worktree created after approval (worktree request/approve/create, refuses without approval), every command auditable/cancellable (Supervisor timeout+process-group kill, tested; JSONL event journal available), default-branch writes blocked (policy Default() denies git.default_branch_write and git.push requires approval).

internal/knowledge/dolt.go: Open() ensures a Dolt repo exists at dataDir (dolt init with fixed --name/--email since this is Punakawan's own internal DB, not human-authored), starts dolt sql-server as a supervised background process on a free local port (via new Supervisor.StartBackground), connects via go-sql-driver/mysql, applies schema (single knowledge_records table: id/type/status/validity_state denormalized + full record as JSON data column - avoids 23 separate normalized tables for the heterogeneous §7.1 entity types). Verified: go test with a real dolt sql-server, lint/build pass. Also added dolt install step to CI.

internal/knowledge/service.go: Store.Put (upsert)/Get/ListByType/Delete over protocol.KnowledgeRecord (generated in M0). ErrNotFound for missing records. 2 tests covering full CRUD + upsert + not-found, run against a real dolt sql-server (skips gracefully if dolt absent). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

Validate() enforces §7.3/§7.4 provenance fields (id scheme, type, title, source, extraction.method, validity.state, verified-requires-verified_by) and is wired into Store.Put(). Covered by provenance_test.go and Put-rejection tests in service_test.go.

Added knowledge_relations index table synced transactionally on Put/Delete; Store.Related(id) does the reverse-lookup join to find records that relate to a given id across repositories. Covered by TestKnowledgeRelationsRoundTrip.

Store.Export/Import in internal/knowledge/importexport.go: JSONL export ordered by id for reproducibility, fail-fast import that routes every record through Put so §7.3/§7.4 provenance validation applies uniformly. Covered by TestExportImportRoundTrip and two rejection tests.

internal/knowledge/staleness.go: ContentHash() produces schema-compliant sha256 digests; Store.CheckStale compares a record's stored source.content_hash against a caller-supplied current hash and transitions validity.state to stale on mismatch via Put. No stored hash means no baseline, so it declines to guess. Covered by 5 tests.

internal/convention.Extract() detects .editorconfig, linter/formatter config (eslint/prettier/golangci-lint/gofmt/stylelint/rustfmt), package manager, monorepo-vs-single layout, and directory naming convention, normalizing into a convention-profile KnowledgeRecord with §27.4-conformant observed/inferred provenance. Scoped to detection only; applying a profile (§27.5) and baseline scaffolding (§27.6) deferred to M3. Covered by 4 tests.

internal/beads wraps the subset of the bd CLI needed (create, dep add, --version) via Supervisor, mirroring the internal/tools/rtk.go thin-wrapper style. Flag choices (--json, --acceptance newline-joined) verified empirically against bd --help. Covered by 4 tests.

internal/tasks.CreateTaskForRequirement builds a TaskContract from a requirement KnowledgeRecord per §10.1 (requirement_id from Id, jira_key from source.external_id when provider=jira), creates the tracking Beads issue via internal/beads, and persists a tracked-by relation back onto the requirement via Store.Put. WireDependency translates the schema's blocks/discovered-from/requires vocabulary onto bd dep add's types. Covered by 6 tests including a full end-to-end round trip.

M2 complete: all 9 deliverables done (Dolt schema, knowledge service, provenance model, cross-repository relations, Beads integration, requirement-to-task mapping, import/export, staleness detection, convention profile extraction). Full make lint && make test green.

Added context-dossier, semar-synthesis, gareng-review, petruk-plan, and bagong-review knowledge record types (§8.1-§8.4, §9.1, §9.2), following the convention-profile precedent of an enum value plus an optional typed payload object on the shared KnowledgeRecord schema. Regenerated Go/TS types.

Added final-plan knowledge record type with the 19 §9.3 fields as an optional payload object, kept separate from semar-synthesis per the plan's own split between the clarification-gate output and the final plan.
internal/roles.SubmitGarengReview validates a required verdict and persists a gareng-review knowledge record via the existing Store.Put/provenance pipeline. Covered by 2 tests.
internal/roles.SubmitPetrukPlan validates a required recommended_solution and persists a petruk-plan knowledge record. Covered by 2 tests.

internal/roles.SubmitSemarSynthesis validates a required goal and persists a semar-synthesis knowledge record (clarification-gate output, §8.1/§9.2). SubmitFinalPlan (§9.3, requires/acceptance_criteria) is also delivered here as the natural companion submission Semar produces at a later workflow stage; the MCP submit_semar_synthesis tool (82m.9) will route to whichever matches the payload. Covered by 4 tests.

Authored prompts/{semar,gareng,petruk,bagong}/prompt.md, each stating the role's §8 responsibilities verbatim, its input context, and its exact output field list matching protocol/knowledge.schema.json's role payload objects. Semar's prompt clearly separates its two distinct submission shapes (semar_synthesis vs final_plan). Bagong's prompt states the raw-evidence-not-summary requirement directly per the plan's own wording.

internal/dossier.Build assembles a context-dossier knowledge record from real workspace/git/knowledge state (source_inventory, existing_implementation_paths via gitops.Status, api_and_data_contracts and relevant_previous_decisions via Store.ListByType) plus caller-supplied intent fields, mirroring internal/convention/extract.go's shape. existing_tests and deployment_path left empty (no clean signal exists yet). Covered by 5 tests.

internal/workflow persists WorkflowRun state via append-only JSONL, mirroring internal/approvals' Store/Current pattern (§18.2's 'load last durable checkpoint'). New() creates the initial 'created' checkpoint; Advance() appends a checkpoint and updates state, rejecting unknown states and any move out of a terminal state (completed/failed/cancelled) but not enforcing an unstated transition graph, since §18.1 only enumerates states, not a specific edge set. Covered by 7 tests.

internal/mcpserver serves the 4 role prompts (embedded from prompts/<role>/prompt.md) and 8 tools (build_context_dossier, submit_gareng_review, submit_petruk_plan, submit_semar_synthesis [routes to synthesis or final_plan], submit_bagong_review, get_workflow_state, advance_workflow, plus create_workflow_run as a necessary addition beyond §28.4's literal list since nothing else starts a run) over the go-sdk's stdio transport, wired via 'punakawan mcp serve'. app.App gained a lazy OpenKnowledge()/Close() so the Dolt server only starts for commands that need it. Verified via 6 in-memory-transport tests plus a real stdio JSON-RPC handshake (initialize/tools list/prompts list) against the built binary.

M3 complete: role contracts, prompt templates, context dossier generation, Gareng/Petruk/Semar/Bagong submission validators, final plan schema, workflow checkpoints, and the MCP server tying them together are all built and tested. Punakawan is now usable locally: 'punakawan mcp serve' exposes the full Semar/Gareng/Petruk/Bagong workflow to any MCP client.

internal/adapters.Gate wraps an adapter Client, checking the adapter manifest's operations[op].approval declaration (§5.4) before invoking execute, reusing the existing internal/approvals Store and mirroring gitops.WorktreeManager's RequestApproval/Approve/Deny pattern generalized from git worktrees to any adapter operation. operationCategory maps op names onto the closest §16.1 ApprovalRecordOperation value (confluence-update/issue-creation/issue-transition/external-write), documented as an interpretive call. Covered by 5 tests using a fake caller (no subprocess needed).

packages/clarification-comments (@punakawan/clarification-comments) renders Semar's already-worded semar_synthesis.open_questions entries into Markdown comment bodies via pure mechanical templating (renderClarificationComment / renderClarificationComments), never composing new wording. OpenQuestion type is derived from the generated KnowledgeRecord type via indexed access rather than hand-retyped. Established the repo's first TS test setup (node:test). Covered by 10 tests.

packages/docling-adapter implements the real Docling Serve REST contract (POST /v1/convert/source per Docling Serve docs), normalizes json_content into source-preserving heading/text/table sections, flags partial_success/error/OCR results as uncertain, and computes sha256 content hashes. 28/28 tests pass against fixture data mirroring the real response shape.

packages/adapter-atlassian implements a real MCP client against mcp.atlassian.com (StreamableHTTPClientTransport, bearer token via ATLASSIAN_MCP_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_CLOUD_ID config), with initialize/execute/shutdown handlers per protocol/adapter.schema.json. addJiraComment declared side_effect:true/approval:required; reads declared side_effect:false. 14/14 tests pass against an in-memory fake MCP server exercising the real wire protocol.

Delivered as part of packages/adapter-atlassian: getJiraIssue and searchJira operations call the real getJiraIssue/searchJiraIssuesUsingJql Atlassian MCP tools and normalize results into source.provider/external_id/version/uri shape, preserving external ID and version for later staleness comparison.

Delivered as part of packages/adapter-atlassian: getConfluencePage and searchConfluence operations call the real getConfluencePage/searchConfluenceUsingCql Atlassian MCP tools and normalize results, preserving Confluence's native version.number for later staleness comparison.

internal/reconcile.CheckSourceStale maps a KnowledgeRecord's source.provider (jira/confluence) to the matching Atlassian adapter operation (atlassian.getJiraIssue/issueIdOrKey, atlassian.getConfluencePage/pageId - cross-checked against packages/adapter-atlassian/src/manifest.ts and adapter.ts, which declare exactly those op/param names), re-fetches via the approval Gate, hashes the raw response, and delegates to knowledge.Store.CheckStale. 5/5 tests pass.

All 7 M4 deliverables complete: Docling Serve adapter, Atlassian MCP adapter (Jira+Confluence read/search/comment), draft comment generation (clarification-comments package), approval-gated writes (internal/adapters.Gate), and source version reconciliation (internal/reconcile), per punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22. Built offline against real API contracts confirmed via Context7 and verified with fixture/fake-server tests, per the mock-verified scoping decision (no live credentials available).

internal/tasks.GenerateGraph batch-creates TaskContracts and wires depends_on edges via LocalKey resolution, reusing createTaskForRequirement/WireDependency. Exposed as MCP tool submit_task_graph. 3 new tests (rejects unknown local_key, rejects duplicate local_key, end-to-end with a real wired blocks dependency verified via bd show) plus a full MCP wire-protocol test (TestSubmitTaskGraphAndListReadyTasks) confirming the blocked task is correctly excluded from list_ready_tasks.

internal/beads.Ready/ClaimReady wrap 'bd ready --json'/'bd ready --claim --json', with flags/JSON shape verified empirically. Exposed as MCP tools list_ready_tasks/claim_ready_task. 11/11 tests pass against real throwaway bd projects. Found and fixed a real bug during MCP integration: ReadyIssue.Labels/Dependencies lacked omitempty, so an issue with no labels failed the MCP output schema (nil slice marshals to JSON null, not []).

internal/taskcontext.Build assembles the §11.2 fresh per-task context (parent requirement, source excerpts, related decisions, previous task outputs, known constraints) from the knowledge store plus caller-supplied task fields, and writes it as this task's task.yaml evidence (§17.2). Exposed as MCP tool build_task_context. 6/6 tests pass against a real Dolt-backed store.

internal/execution.StartTaskExecution/FinishTaskExecution orchestrate §11.1 steps 1-4 and 10 (worktree creation via gitops.WorktreeManager, evidence bundle/journal open, final status event, worktree removal). Exposed as MCP tools start_task_execution/finish_task_execution, reconstructing session state across the two stateless calls from deterministic paths (gitops.WorktreePath, evidence.OpenJournal). 3/3 unit tests plus a full MCP wire-protocol lifecycle test (TestTaskExecutionLifecycle: start -> write_file -> check_diff -> commit_task -> finish) all pass.

internal/fileops.WriteFile checks policy.AllowsFilesystemWrite and rejects path traversal (relative-clean-based, not clamping, so escapes are rejected rather than silently redirected) before writing within a task's worktree. Exposed as MCP tool write_file. 5/5 tests pass covering traversal, absolute-path rejection, and policy-deny enforcement.

internal/fileops.BulkCreateFiles applies WriteFile's checks per file with best-effort (non-transactional) per-file results. Exposed as MCP tool bulk_create_files. Covered by fileops's 5-test suite including TestBulkCreateFilesReportsPerFileOutcome (mixed success/policy-deny/traversal in one call).

internal/diffcheck.Check stages a worktree's pending changes (git add -A), checks each changed file against policy and a documented heuristic secret-pattern set (AWS keys, PEM headers, password/secret-key assignments), and writes diff.patch evidence (§17.2). Exposed as MCP tool check_diff. 4/4 tests pass: clean change allowed, policy-denied path blocked, AWS-key-shaped secret blocked, new+deleted files both captured.

internal/testrun.Run executes caller-specified commands via the existing tools.Supervisor (no reimplementation of its safety controls), continuing past a failing command so all results are captured, and writes tests.json evidence (§17.2). Exposed as MCP tool run_tests. 4/4 tests pass against real subprocesses.

internal/openapicheck.Check diffs two OpenAPI specs via the real oasdiff library (github.com/oasdiff/oasdiff/diff + checker, API confirmed via Context7 plus direct go doc/source reading for the aggregator checker.CheckBackwardCompatibility, which was not documented by Context7 alone) and classifies breaking vs non-breaking changes, writing api-diff.json evidence (§13.4, §17.2). Exposed as MCP tool check_openapi_compatibility. 5/5 tests pass against real fixture specs (no-change, compatible addition, breaking required-param removal).

gitops.WorktreeManager.CommitTask requires a passed diff-check result and a punakawan/-prefixed task branch (defense-in-depth per §15.4's 'no direct edits on default branch') before staging and committing, then records base and resulting commit SHAs. Worktree.BaseSHA is now captured at creation time via a new Inspector.HeadSHA method. Exposed as MCP tool commit_task. 3/3 new gitops tests pass, plus the full MCP lifecycle test exercises a real commit end-to-end.

internal/tasks.ReportDiscoveredWork extends the existing createTaskForRequirement helper (refactored to share logic, zero duplication) to force discovered_from and add 'discovered'+'needs-semar-review' bd labels per §10.4's discovery rule, letting Semar find discovered work via bd list --label without a new review-queue subsystem. Exposed as MCP tool report_discovered_task. 8/8 tests pass, including verifying the actual persisted bd labels via bd show.

All 11 M6 deliverables complete: task graph generation, ready/claim task selection, per-task fresh context, worktree-lifecycle orchestration, file-editing abstraction (single+bulk), diff/secret checking, test execution, OpenAPI compatibility checking, commit creation with git-safety guards, and discovered-task handling, per punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §10-§11. All 13 new MCP tools wired into internal/mcpserver and verified end-to-end over the real MCP wire protocol (submit_task_graph+list_ready_tasks dependency-blocking test, and a full start->write_file->check_diff->commit_task->finish lifecycle test), plus 164/164 repo-wide Go tests passing.

Registry.Gate lazily starts an adapter process, discovers its manifest via the new §5.3 capabilities RPC (added to prototypeAdapter.ts/docling-adapter/adapter-atlassian), completes initialize, and memoizes the Gate; Registry.Close shuts down spawned processes. Wired into app.App.Load/Close. New call_adapter_operation MCP tool routes through Gate.Call, enforcing manifest-declared approval gating. Verified: internal/adapters (9 tests, incl. 4 new registry_test.go cases: happy path, memoization, unknown id, Close-shuts-down-process) + 2 new MCP wire-protocol tests in tools_adapters_test.go (end-to-end call against the real spawned prototype adapter, unknown-adapter-id error) all pass; full go build/vet/gofmt clean; go test ./... = 188 passed/28 packages.

internal/jiraworkflow.Config (Load/Default/ShouldSkip/EstimateHours/ValidateStoryPoints) implements the org-specific status/estimation split grounded in the epic's own research (statusCategory is generic, raw status names/estimation scales are not). Reviewed the subagent's implementation directly: correct empty-scale-means-no-validation and zero-ratio-means-unconfigured semantics, case-insensitive ShouldSkip. Verified: 10/10 tests in jiraworkflow_test.go pass against test/fixtures/jira-workflow.yaml.

atlassian.getTransitionsForJiraIssue and atlassian.transitionJiraIssue added to operations.ts/manifest.ts/adapter.ts, transition-required-before-status-name design as specified. Reviewed diff directly (manifest declares transitionJiraIssue approval:required, getTransitionsForJiraIssue side_effect:false; adapter.ts dispatch matches manifest op names exactly). Verified independently (not just per subagent report): pnpm --filter @punakawan/adapter-atlassian typecheck/build/test all clean, 34/34 tests pass.

atlassian.editJiraIssueFields, atlassian.addWorklog, atlassian.getIssueTypeFieldMeta added per spec (arbitrary fields passthrough, no hardcoded story-point field id). Reviewed diff directly, approval gating correct on both write ops. Verified independently: pnpm --filter @punakawan/adapter-atlassian typecheck/build/test all clean, 34/34 tests pass.

atlassian.createJiraSubtask added with exact-match (trimmed/whitespace-collapsed/case-insensitive) dedup against existing children via searchJira JQL, only creating genuinely-new candidates. Deviation from the task description, flagged and accepted: issueTypeName is caller-supplied rather than internally discovered via getJiraProjectIssueTypesMetadata, since that tool isn't implemented anywhere in this codebase (confirmed by the subagent reading the full existing operations.ts first) - noted for whoever picks up .7/.8. Verified independently: pnpm --filter @punakawan/adapter-atlassian typecheck/build/test all clean, 34/34 tests pass.

request_jira_clarification posts a comment via atlassian.addJiraComment, then (only when jira-workflow.yaml configures clarification_status) looks up real workflow transitions via atlassian.getTransitionsForJiraIssue and applies the matching one via atlassian.transitionJiraIssue, erroring clearly if none matches. Comment body is pre-rendered upstream (via packages/clarification-comments' new CLI entry point, added this task) rather than reimplemented in Go, avoiding duplicated templating logic. Verified: 4 new unit tests (tools_jiraclarify_test.go) against a fake adapter caller covering post+transition happy path, unconfigured-status skip, no-matching-transition error, and missing-approval error; go build/vet/gofmt clean; go test ./... all pass; clarification-comments CLI smoke-tested directly and its own 10/10 tests still pass.

check_jira_skippable reads a requirement's raw Jira status (stored on its KnowledgeRecord by adapter-atlassian's normalizeJiraIssue) against jiraworkflow.Config.ShouldSkip - implemented as a separate pre-check tool rather than modifying internal/tasks.GenerateGraph directly, since silently dropping items there would complicate its already-shipped, tested dependency-wiring semantics for every caller. sync_jira_subtasks wraps atlassian.createJiraSubtask (dedup logic already lives in .4's work) with approval gating. Verified: 4 new tests (2 real MCP wire-protocol tests seeding KnowledgeRecords with jira-sourced status + jira-workflow.yaml config; 2 unit tests against a fake adapter caller for the create/skip/no-approval paths); go build/vet/gofmt clean; go test ./... all pass.

update_jira_task_progress derives original estimate hours via jiraworkflow.Config.EstimateHours(story_points) unless original_estimate_hours is given explicitly (explicit wins, per user decision), writes it as Jira's native timetracking.originalEstimate duration string (fractional hours split into Nh Mm rather than an undocumented decimal), adds a worklog entry, and posts a comment - each of the three independently optional and independently approval-gated. Verified: 6 new unit tests against a fake adapter caller (points-derived estimate, explicit-overrides-points, no-estimate-when-ratio-unconfigured, worklog+comment together, missing-approval error, duration formatting incl. whole/fractional/zero hours); go build/vet/gofmt clean; go test ./... all pass.

All 8 children closed. Acceptance criteria met (offline/mock-verified scope, per user decision): (1) call_adapter_operation reaches a real spawned adapter process over the live MCP server, verified end-to-end; (2) check_jira_skippable correctly excludes Jira-sourced requirements whose stored status matches a configured skip_statuses entry; (3) request_jira_clarification posts a comment then transitions via a real getTransitionsForJiraIssue lookup, failing clearly if no matching transition exists; (4) createJiraSubtask/sync_jira_subtasks dedupe by normalized summary against existing children before creating; (5) update_jira_task_progress fills original estimate from story points via the configured ratio unless an explicit estimate is given (which wins), and independently adds worklog/comment. Also fixed the epic's own foundational gap: M4's adapter/Gate infrastructure is now actually reachable from the running MCP server via the new §5.3 capabilities message and Registry. All work committed in 10 scoped commits; full go build/vet/gofmt/test and pnpm -r test clean throughout.

mcpClient.ts's buildAuthorizationHeader branches on whether ATLASSIAN_EMAIL is set: personal API token (email present) -> Basic base64(email:token); service-account key (no email) -> Bearer token - both configurable, matching the user's explicit ask to support either credential type. Confirmed via two independent WebFetch reads of the official Atlassian docs (configuring-authentication-via-api-token) that these header formats are not interchangeable. Verified: 4 new tests in mcpClient.test.ts (email presence/absence, both header formats incl. base64 round-trip); all 38 adapter-atlassian tests pass; typecheck/build clean.

Added `punakawan approvals list/approve/deny` CLI (cmd/punakawan/approvals_cmd.go), backed by a new generic internal/approvals.Store.Resolve method that resolves by id/status/approver alone, working for both worktree-creation and adapter-operation approval records (unlike the existing worktree-only approve/deny commands). Deliberately CLI-only, not an MCP tool: per §16.2, approved_by is the human, distinct from requested_by (the AI role) - exposing approve/deny over MCP would let the same connected client grant its own pending request. Verified: 3 new approvals package tests (approve, unknown-id error, already-resolved error) + 1 new CLI test exercising list/approve/deny against a real adapter-operation-shaped approval id end-to-end; full go build/vet/gofmt/test clean (all 24 packages).

Global macOS installer is implemented: installs prerequisites, builds/packages Punakawan and Atlassian adapter, stores credentials in user config outside git, writes global adapter config, derives cloud ID from site host, and registers a user-scope Claude Code MCP server. bash syntax, repository build, tests, and lint verified; README now documents Jira-first setup and use.

Added elicitation-first MCP approval flow shared by all adapter-writing tools. Approval id is scoped only to run_id, so all operations and adapters reuse one human grant. Unsupported clients get the exact CLI fallback; accept/decline/cross-operation/cross-adapter paths are tested. Full Go suite (except environment-only TestDoctor), TS tests, lint, and build pass.

Gate and elicitation fallback errors now include the exact punakawan approvals approve command and approval id. Server instructions, MCP tool descriptions, installer output, and README document inline approval and CLI fallback.

Added scripts/configure-agent.sh wizard with Codex, Claude Code, both, generic MCP, and skip choices; Codex PATH/ChatGPT-bundle detection; user-scope idempotent registration; dry-run and automation overrides; portable generic mcp-config.json; integration summary; missing-client recovery; README/install updates; and isolated shell tests wired into make test. bash -n, wizard tests, gofmt, go vet, and diff checks pass.

Implemented live Atlassian MCP tools/list discovery and caching, fail-fast permission/scope diagnostics, API-token rejection translation, installer and README guidance, and fake-server coverage. Verified against the configured account: only Teamwork Graph tools are advertised and getTeamworkGraphObject rejects API-token access, confirming there is no safe Jira fallback. Adapter typecheck, 46 tests, build, installer syntax, and diff checks pass.

Replaced hosted Rovo MCP transport with direct Jira Cloud REST v3 and Confluence REST. Added unscoped personal, scoped personal, and scoped service-account token modes; direct endpoint/auth/error handling; ADF write mapping; enhanced JQL search; request-level tests; installer migration; documentation and ADR updates. Verified 49 adapter tests, full repository tests, lint, build, package, and live read-only issue plus JQL calls against the configured Jira account.

Installer now migrates legacy ATLASSIAN_MCP_TOKEN credentials and env_passthrough to direct REST keys with recoverable backups, derives scoped mode from whether an email is present, and is idempotent. Applied migration to current installation, reran installer twice, doctor passed, and live direct Jira issue plus JQL reads returned HTTP 200.
Fixed Dolt server reuse/startup diagnostics and compacted Jira plus MCP responses; full tests, lint, build, package, and live Codex launcher smoke passed.

Implemented compact Jira comments, links, epic context, workspace-confined attachment operations, broad editJiraIssue, and interactive per-run approvals; tests, lint, builds, packaging, and live-safe reads pass.

Fixed via marklassian-based markdownAdf() replacing plainTextAdf() across all 4 free-text call sites (comment, worklog comment, 2x description). New test asserts real heading/bulletList/strong ADF nodes and that literal ## / ** do not survive into flattened text. 64/64 adapter-atlassian tests pass, full pnpm -r build/test and go test ./... clean.

Fixed via decodeHtmlEntities() applied before markdownToAdf() in operations.ts markdownAdf(). New test posts '-> ... &' and asserts the flattened ADF text reads '-> ... &'. 65/65 adapter-atlassian tests pass, full pnpm -r build/test clean. Upstream root cause (which client/session sent pre-escaped text) not identified - this is a defensive fix at the render boundary, not a fix of the unknown upstream source.

Implemented via bagong_review.blocking_findings (protocol/knowledge.schema.json, mirrors gareng_review's existing field) plus the advance_workflow completion gate that refuses to complete a run while its latest Bagong review has unresolved blocking_findings. Severity model is binary (blocking vs. non-blocking), matching the existing gareng_review precedent rather than inventing a new taxonomy.

Implemented: new reopen_task MCP tool + beads.ReopenIssue (shells out to bd reopen) for reopening a regression in already-completed work, pairing with the pre-existing report_discovered_task for creating new follow-up work. advance_workflow to completed is refused while blocking_findings are unresolved, satisfying the 'delivery cannot be marked complete' acceptance criterion.

No new Go tool needed: build_task_context already re-pulls the parent requirement fresh (independent of Petruk's own summary). Server Instructions now explicitly direct Bagong's agent to call it fresh plus read the task's evidence bundle files directly, rather than trusting task.yaml alone.

Agent-reasoning deliverables (ADR-0016: punakawan does not reason, the connected agent does) over data the core already exposes: check_diff/run_tests/check_openapi_compatibility already write diff.patch/tests.json/api-diff.json into the evidence bundle; migrations are ordinary files visible in the same diff.patch. Server Instructions now direct Bagong to read these directly as part of its independent review. No new core infra was missing for these.

Agent-reasoning deliverables (ADR-0016: punakawan does not reason, the connected agent does) over data the core already exposes: check_diff/run_tests/check_openapi_compatibility already write diff.patch/tests.json/api-diff.json into the evidence bundle; migrations are ordinary files visible in the same diff.patch. Server Instructions now direct Bagong to read these directly as part of its independent review. No new core infra was missing for these.

Agent-reasoning deliverables (ADR-0016: punakawan does not reason, the connected agent does) over data the core already exposes: check_diff/run_tests/check_openapi_compatibility already write diff.patch/tests.json/api-diff.json into the evidence bundle; migrations are ordinary files visible in the same diff.patch. Server Instructions now direct Bagong to read these directly as part of its independent review. No new core infra was missing for these.

Agent-reasoning deliverables (ADR-0016: punakawan does not reason, the connected agent does) over data the core already exposes: check_diff/run_tests/check_openapi_compatibility already write diff.patch/tests.json/api-diff.json into the evidence bundle; migrations are ordinary files visible in the same diff.patch. Server Instructions now direct Bagong to read these directly as part of its independent review. No new core infra was missing for these.

Agent-reasoning deliverables (ADR-0016: punakawan does not reason, the connected agent does) over data the core already exposes: check_diff/run_tests/check_openapi_compatibility already write diff.patch/tests.json/api-diff.json into the evidence bundle; migrations are ordinary files visible in the same diff.patch. Server Instructions now direct Bagong to read these directly as part of its independent review. No new core infra was missing for these.
added ingest_jira_requirement tool; build_task_context/submit_task_graph descriptions + not-found errors now name the prerequisite too
Advance() now enforces §9's transition graph (forwardTransitions) instead of accepting any non-terminal next state; blocked/failed/cancelled remain universal escape hatches
Resolve() now rejects an agent role name (semar/gareng/petruk/bagong) as its own approver; documented as a floor not real auth - MCP elicitation remains the actual human-in-the-loop boundary

All 4 acceptance items addressed: inline preconditions (zvt), gate mechanics + enum values on tool descriptions (this commit), and prerequisite-naming error messages (zvt). No separate runbook resource added - serverInstructions already states the canonical call sequence and advance_workflow now states its enum inline, which covers the same need without a new resource type.
Added policy.ApprovalsPolicy.Scope ('run' default or 'day'), threaded through Registry/Gate.scopeKey; opt-in via policy.yaml, no behavior change by default

Any elicitation error now falls back to the same clean ACTION REQUIRED message; removed the dead unsupported-substring allowlist that let unmatched errors (e.g. a client-side zod validation error) leak through raw
BuildInput.Previous (loaded from the task's own task.yaml evidence) fills in omitted scope/AC/DoD/expected-files/symbols/tests fields on a resume call; caller only sends what actually changed
protocol/capsule.schema.json + generated ContextCapsule type; internal/capsule.Digest computes the deterministic §6.3 digest, nil/empty-slice normalized
protocol/capsule.schema.json + generated ContextCapsule type; internal/capsule.Digest computes the deterministic §6.3 digest, nil/empty-slice normalized

Implemented capsule-gated tool calls per the user's chosen design: request_capsule builds+digests+persists an immutable ContextCapsule (internal/capsule), rejecting forbidden knowledge-record types and forbidden tools for the target role. submit_gareng_review/submit_petruk_plan/submit_bagong_review now require a capsule_id issued for that exact role, verified via requireCapsuleForRole (existence, role match, digest integrity). 281 tests passing, no regressions.

All 3 children complete: ContextCapsule schema (ef8), deterministic digest (ag2), and capsule-gated tool-layer enforcement (ow9). Gareng/Petruk/Bagong now require a role-scoped capsule to submit their review/plan, closing the isolation gap this milestone targeted.

Added internal/evidence.Ledger (append-only JSONL of protocol.EvidenceRecord) and RecordArtifact, wired into check_diff/run_tests/check_openapi_compatibility right after each writes its bundle file. New list_task_evidence MCP tool enumerates a task's recorded evidence structurally instead of by file-naming convention.

Added internal/syncqueue.Queue (append-only JSONL, fold-latest-per-id like approvals.Store) and wired it into adapters.Gate.Call/Registry: a write that fails after passing approval is recorded with params, attempt count, and conflict detection against other pending entries for the same adapter/op/issue. New list_jira_sync_queue and retry_jira_sync_entry MCP tools find and replay queued failures. Nothing here blocks or is called by commit_task/finish_task_execution.

Both children complete: structured evidence references (s12, via internal/evidence.Ledger + list_task_evidence) and an outbound Jira sync retry/conflict queue (nbz, via internal/syncqueue). Closes the two concrete gaps identified against the plan; the rest of M2's scope (BD-as-source-of-truth, Jira-as-optional-adapter) was already implemented.

Added protocol.GitCapabilities/GitExecutionPolicy schemas and Inspector.DetectCapabilities/gitops.MergeExecutionPolicy implementing §7.4's 'detected ∩ repository policy ∩ user permission' merge rule. PR-related capabilities stay false with a limitation note pending AEP-M4's provider adapter.

Added protocol.GitCapabilities/GitExecutionPolicy schemas and Inspector.DetectCapabilities/gitops.MergeExecutionPolicy implementing §7.4's 'detected ∩ repository policy ∩ user permission' merge rule. PR-related capabilities stay false with a limitation note pending AEP-M4's provider adapter.

Added protocol.GitCapabilities/GitExecutionPolicy schemas and Inspector.DetectCapabilities/gitops.MergeExecutionPolicy implementing §7.4's 'detected ∩ repository policy ∩ user permission' merge rule. PR-related capabilities stay false with a limitation note pending AEP-M4's provider adapter.

All 3 children complete: remote/provider detection (hp7), default branch/push detection (a44), and the GitCapabilities/GitExecutionPolicy schema plus detected-intersect-policy-intersect-user merge (nzg). Note: PR read/write/comment permission detection (also listed in §7.2's checklist) intentionally stays false-with-a-limitation-note pending AEP-M4's provider adapter, which this epic unblocks.

Implemented packages/github-adapter following adapter-atlassian's structure: manifest-declared operations (getPullRequest/getPullRequestFiles/getPullRequestChecks/listPullRequestComments reads; createPullRequest/addLabels/requestReviewers/replyToReviewComment/resolveReviewThread approval-gated writes), a direct REST+GraphQL client (Bearer auth via GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN, GHES-aware base URLs), and normalized shapes for PR/diff/CI/comment payloads. resolveReviewThread uses GraphQL since GitHub's REST API has no thread-resolution endpoint. 23 TS tests passing, whole pnpm workspace builds clean.

Implemented create_pr (templates caller-supplied PR body sections per §8.1, checks preconditions via DetectCapabilities, reports the specific unavailability reason instead of erroring) and push_task_branch (never force-pushes, gated via MergeExecutionPolicy). Added gitops.RepoSlug and gitops.DefaultExecutionPolicy to support them. Repository-policy layer is pinned to allow-everything for now - filed punokawan-06g.1 to wire in a real policy.yaml Git section. 323 tests passing, no regressions.

review_pr + submit_pr_review_findings shipped: fetch-only PR context bundle (metadata/files/checks/comments) gated by explicit_trigger, per ADR-0016 the tool never judges the content itself - the calling agent runs the existing capsule/role pipeline and submits Semar's result, persisted append-only via internal/prreview.Store.

fetch_unresolved_pr_comments + resolve_review_thread shipped: fetch side uses GitHub's GraphQL listUnresolvedReviewThreads (REST has no resolution-state field) gated by explicit_trigger; resolve side requires an explicit per-call allow=true, mirroring push_task_branch's permission field, since §8.3 says threads are never auto-resolved.

Added superseded_by to protocol.KnowledgeRecord (knowledge.schema.json §10.4) and Store.Supersede(id, supersededBy): marks a record superseded in place via the existing Put path rather than deleting it.

Added .punakawan/events/knowledge-events.jsonl (§10.1/§10.2): Store now emits a put event on every Put and an additional supersede event on every Supersede, readable back via Store.Events() for external tailing/audit.

Added Store.ExportYAML/ImportYAML (PortableExport{Records}) alongside the existing JSONL Export/Import, giving §10.1's portable.yaml a full round trip through the same Put-based provenance validation as every other write path.

All three AEP-M5 children shipped: supersededBy field + Supersede API (pkv), knowledge-events.jsonl audit trail (452), portable YAML export/import (ei9). Durable knowledge core now covers all four §10.1 storage layers (Dolt canonical, JSONL events, YAML portable, BM25 index still pending in AEP-M6).

internal/search.Tokenize implements the technical tokenizer: splits camelCase/PascalCase/snake_case/kebab-case/dotted-package/colon-coordinate/slash-path tokens while always preserving the original and lowercased whole token, and never splits digits off adjoining letters (java8time stays whole) or explodes acronym runs (URLParser -> URL, Parser not U/R/L/Parser).

internal/search.DetectIdentifiers recognizes CVE/GHSA/Sonar-rule/Jira-key/BD-task-id/PR-number/git-hash/version-string/Maven-coordinate/npm-package/Go-module/file-path/API-route/Java-class-or-method/symbol patterns, ordered most-specific-first so e.g. a Sonar rule (java:S3776) is not mistaken for a generic Maven coordinate. Wired into scoreResult as the +100 exact-identifier bonus that outranks every BM25 result per §11.10.

Added github.com/blevesearch/bleve/v2, a custom punakawan_technical analyzer backed directly by Tokenize, and internal/search.Index (Open/Close/IndexRecord/DeleteRecord/Rebuild) over §11.4's document model (title/summary/content/aliases/tags/paths/symbols/identifiers, plus type/project/repository/module/trustLevel as keyword fields). §11.5's field weights (identifiers 12.0 down to content 1.0) are applied as per-field query boosts in search.go's BM25F DisjunctionQuery, since Bleve has no static per-field weight on the mapping itself. protocol.KnowledgeRecord gained summary/content/aliases/tags/scope fields to give the document model something to index.

Shipped search_knowledge MCP tool: full §11.2 pipeline (BM25F first, fuzzy fallback only on zero hits) plus §11.10's exact-identifier/alias/scope/trust ranking bonuses, §11.9's one-hop relation expansion, and a per-result Explanation for §11.13's explain-match, all returned via match.kind/fields/terms. The tool resyncs the disposable Bleve index (an idempotent full re-upsert via Rebuild) before every query so results are never stale, since no separate refresh_index tool exists yet in this milestone's scope.

All four AEP-M6 children shipped in internal/search: technical tokenizer (44o), exact-identifier detection (69a), Bleve BM25F integration with §11.5 field weights (6hi), and the search_knowledge MCP tool with full ranking + explain-match (s1z). Local, embeddings-free, no external model calls, per §11.1's search goals. Unblocks AEP-M7 (punokawan-ovm).
